name: First Step Trans/Thomas Buses
districts_or_customers: NYC DOE (Office of Pupil Transportation) - Brooklyn-area school routes
recent_news: First Step Trans and Thomas Buses Inc operate as affiliated entities at the same Brooklyn (Coney Island/Gravesend) depot under common owner Vincent D'Amato; both listed in NYC DOE school-bus operator rosters; Birdeye/Yelp reviews are negative on service quality (~1.8 stars) but the business appears active in 2025-2026.
fit_notes: Small independent NYC DOE contractor pair (~50 employees combined) under single owner D'Amato at one Brooklyn depot - classic NYC tuck-in profile if Summit pursues NYC OPT footprint; small scale and reputational issues mean it is a bolt-on, not anchor.

name: Geri Transportation Co Inc
districts_or_customers: Formerly NYC DOE Brooklyn school routes
recent_news: Multiple Yelp listings for Geri Transportation Co Inc at 2542 W 13th St, Brooklyn (phone 718-266-9400) are marked CLOSED as of 2025-2026 updates; historical member of Empire State Transportation Workers' Comp Trust; no acquisition announcement found.
fit_notes: Appears defunct/closed - not a viable tuck-in target. Drop from active pipeline; if interested, could probe NYC DOE OPT for any successor entity at the Brooklyn depot.

name: Pace Transportation Inc
recent_news: Family-run Pace Transportation has provided school bus service from Pittston/West Pittston since 1987; long-standing Wyoming Area School District contractor; no M&A or sale activity found in public sources.
fit_notes: ~38-year-old independent family operator in NEPA's Wyoming Valley with stable district relationships - solid Summit tuck-in candidate (Summit/Durham has heavy PA presence); founder Robert Pace likely at/near succession age making this a credible approach target.

name: Thomas Buses Inc
owner_or_parent: independent (affiliated with First Step Trans Inc - same owner)
districts_or_customers: NYC DOE (Office of Pupil Transportation) - Brooklyn-area school routes
recent_news: Thomas Buses Inc and First Step Trans operate as the same effective business under Vincent D'Amato at 2859 W 37th St, Brooklyn - reported as 27+ years in business; listed on NYC DOE school bus operator rosters; not a separate target from rank #398.
fit_notes: DUPLICATE of rank #398 - same owner, same Brooklyn depot, same phone. De-duplicate in pipeline; treat as one combined NYC DOE bolt-on opportunity of ~100 employees rather than two separate targets.

name: K & K Bus Line & K Bus Line
districts_or_customers: Schoharie County area (likely Cobleskill-Richmondville CSD or neighboring districts) - charter/coach in addition to school
recent_news: K & K Bus Line operates a charter/school bus business out of 24 E Main St, Richmondville NY (Schoharie County); active in 2025-2026 per Yelp; no acquisition or PE ownership signals found. Note Cobleskill-Richmondville CSD operates its own 61-bus fleet so K&K likely serves neighboring small Schoharie districts and charter work.
fit_notes: Small independent rural Upstate NY operator (~45 employees) in Schoharie County - geographically isolated from Summit's denser NY clusters; would be a true micro tuck-in only if Summit is building a Capital Region/Mohawk Valley footprint. Low priority absent regional strategy.
